# Conventions

## claude-code-knowledge skill files are the best-practices corpus for daily review

op-daily-review.js reads all files under the claude-code-knowledge skill directory as the 'best practices' input to the Opus prompt. The skill therefore serves a dual purpose: documentation for humans AND context for the AI reviewer. When updating claude-code-knowledge (adding guides, removing stale rules), you are directly changing what the daily review agent knows. Out-of-date content in that skill degrades review quality.

_Source: `CLAUDE.md`_

_Tags: `daily-review`, `claude-code-knowledge`, `skills`, `opus`, `quality`_

## Tool-Specific Knowledge Files with Usage Analytics

Tool knowledge files (in `/tools/` subdirectory) include YAML frontmatter with total_invocations, sessions_used, success_rate, and last_used timestamp. These metrics enable usage-based discovery of which tools are most effective across Claude Code sessions.

_Source: `/Users/du/Workspace/open-pulse/.claude/knowledge/tools/`_

_Tags: `knowledge-metadata`, `tool-usage`, `analytics`_

## Knowledge Vault Organization with 5 Subdirectories

The knowledge vault uses a flat file structure with 5 subdirectories: components (153 files), instincts (52), notes (1), patterns (106), tools (28), totaling 352 markdown files in 1.4MB. Components are further organized by type prefix (skill:, agent:, rule:). Each subdirectory serves a distinct purpose in the knowledge discovery system.

_Source: `/Users/du/Workspace/open-pulse/.claude/knowledge/`_

_Tags: `knowledge-system`, `file-organization`, `vault-structure`_

## Knowledge extraction creates duplicates with LLM caption variation

The post-ingest knowledge extraction pipeline (op-knowledge.js via Haiku) can generate semantically identical entries with different capitalization, creating data quality issues. This happens because: (1) each prompt's extraction runs independently without consulting existing entries, (2) Haiku has no constraint to match prior capitalization patterns, (3) deduplication only checks exact matches. Result: 232 active entries with 28 case-insensitive duplicates. Mitigation: (a) pass getExistingTitles(db, projectId) to extraction prompt as context, or (b) run periodic lowercase normalization + merge pass.

_Source: `src/op-knowledge.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge-extraction`, `haiku`, `deduplication`, `data-pipeline`_

## Knowledge Entry Dedup via Database Query for Existing Titles

Knowledge entries are deduplicated by the getExistingTitles helper, which queries all active entry titles for a project from the database. The extraction logic then checks if each extracted title matches any existing title before upserting. Current implementation case-sensitive; for production should use LOWER() for case-insensitive dedup or fuzzy matching for near-duplicate detection.

_Source: `src/db/knowledge-entries.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge-extraction`, `deduplication`, `database-pattern`_

## Knowledge title canonicalization for deduplication

When extracting knowledge entries, normalize titles to a consistent case (recommend sentence-case: first letter upper, rest lower) before checking for existing entries or inserting new ones. This prevents the LLM from creating semantically identical entries with different capitalizations. Implement in: (1) the LLM extraction prompt to request consistent capitalization, (2) the `upsertKnowledgeEntry()` dedup check to compare normalized titles, or (3) both for defense in depth.

_Source: `src/op-knowledge.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge-extraction`, `llm-output-normalization`, `dedup`_

## Knowledge Entry Deduplication by Title Query

Find duplicate knowledge entries using: SELECT title, COUNT(*) as count FROM knowledge_entries WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY title HAVING COUNT(*) > 1. Useful for maintenance and cleanup after bulk knowledge extraction to identify entries that may need merging or consolidation.

_Source: `/Users/du/Workspace/open-pulse/src/db/knowledge-entries.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge`, `deduplication`, `maintenance`_

## Knowledge Extraction Hook Injection Pattern

Knowledge extraction is decoupled from ingestion via setKnowledgeHook(extractFn, config) in op-ingest.js. The hook function and config are stored in module-level variables (_extractKnowledge, _knowledgeConfig). During prompt ingestion, the hook is invoked with event data to extract knowledge entries post-ingest. This pattern allows extraction logic to evolve independently without tight coupling to op-ingest.js.

_Source: `/Users/du/Workspace/open-pulse/src/op-ingest.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge`, `architecture`, `hooks`, `decoupling`_

## Knowledge Entry Categorization with Project Scoping

Knowledge entries are categorized (e.g., architecture, convention, error_pattern, footgun) and linked to a project_id in the knowledge_entries table. This enables project-scoped knowledge queries and prevents mixing insights from different projects. Category filtering in the UI allows users to browse knowledge by type (e.g., 'show me all footguns for this project').

_Source: `src/db/knowledge-entries.js`_

_Tags: `knowledge`, `categorization`, `project-scoping`_

## Stateful Feedback Loop UI Pattern (Accept/Dismiss and Promote/Revert)

Frontend modules implement feedback systems where users interact with system-generated suggestions or patterns. Two manifestations: daily-reviews (accept/dismiss Opus suggestions) and auto-evolves (promote/revert observer patterns). Pattern: 1) list endpoint returns items with status and confidence metadata, 2) render list/detail views with status-based styling (badges, confidence bars), 3) action buttons trigger async PUT to update status, 4) frontend manages loading/error states during request, 5) on success, update item status and optionally refetch list. Both use status fields to track user responses, confidence scores for reliability indication, and timestamps for auditability.

_Source: `public/modules/daily-reviews.js,public/modules/auto-evolves.js`_

_Tags: `convention`, `frontend`, `async-ui`, `feedback-loops`_

## Tab Navigation Pattern Within Frontend Route Modules

Multi-tab interfaces within a single route define a TABS array with `{ key, label }` objects. Track the active tab and update `location.hash` when tabs are clicked (e.g., `location.hash = '#knowledge/entries'`) to persist navigation state. On tab change, re-render or show/hide panels based on the active tab key. This enables bookmarkable tab state within a single route.

_Source: `public/modules/knowledge.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `navigation`, `tabs`, `routing`, `ui-state`_

## Mount Function Parameter Object Signature

Frontend route module `mount(containerElement, options)` receives an optional second parameter structured as `{ params: { id: '...', key: '...' } }`. Use destructuring with defaults: `mount(el, { params = {} } = {})` to safely access route parameters. The params object contains variables extracted from location.hash (e.g., `#route/detail/123` → `params.id`). Always check params before use to handle cases where the route has no parameters.

_Source: `public/modules/auto-evolves.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `module-signature`, `routing`, `parameters`_

## Empty State Error UI Pattern in Frontend Modules

When API calls fail or return empty results, frontend modules render an error/empty state by creating a div with class `empty-state`, setting `style.padding` and `style.textContent` to display a user-friendly message. The empty-state class provides centered flexbox layout. Use this pattern consistently across all list/detail views to handle errors gracefully without showing blank screens.

_Source: `public/modules/learning-projects.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `ui`, `error-handling`, `ux`, `empty-state`_

## Chart.js Instance Lifecycle Management in Frontend Modules

Frontend modules rendering Chart.js maintain a module-level `charts` array to track all instances. Before re-rendering or during unmount, call a `destroyCharts()` function that iterates through the array calling `.destroy()` on each instance, then resets the array. This prevents memory leaks from lingering chart objects when users navigate away. Store each new Chart instance in the array after creation.

_Source: `public/modules/learning-projects.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `chart.js`, `memory-management`, `lifecycle`, `cleanup`_

## Status Badge Color Coding in Frontend Modules

Frontend modules use consistent color codes for status badges: active=#6c5ce7 (purple), promoted=#00b894 (green), reverted=#e17055 (red), cleanup=#e17055 (red), adoption=#00b894 (green), update=#fdcb6e (yellow), agent_creation=#6c5ce7 (purple). Define as const colors = {...} object and reference when rendering badge elements.

_Source: `public/modules/daily-reviews.js, public/modules/auto-evolves.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `styling`, `UI`, `constants`, `visualization`_

## Chart Instance Lifecycle Management in Frontend Modules

When creating Chart.js instances in frontend modules, maintain a module-level charts array: let charts = []. When creating a chart, push to this array. In export function unmount(), iterate and call chart.destroy() on each to prevent memory leaks and 'Chart already exists' errors on re-mount. This is critical for modules that are mounted/unmounted repeatedly.

_Source: `public/modules/learning-projects.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `charts`, `memory-management`, `cleanup`, `lifecycle`_

## Tab Navigation State Within Module Views

Multi-tab modules (e.g., knowledge.js with Entries/Scan) should: 1) Define const TABS = [{ key: 'entries', label: 'Entries' }, { key: 'scan', label: 'Scan' }]. 2) Track let activeTab state. 3) Render tab buttons with click handlers updating location.hash = '#knowledge/' + tab.key. 4) In mount(), parse hash or query params to determine active tab and render accordingly. 5) Provide export unmount() for cleanup.

_Source: `public/modules/knowledge.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `navigation`, `tabs`, `state-management`, `routing`_

## Empty State UI Pattern for Frontend Modules

When an API call fails or returns no data, render an empty-state div: var errEl = document.createElement('div'); errEl.className = 'empty-state'; errEl.style.padding = '20px'; errEl.style.textAlign = 'center'; errEl.textContent = 'Failed to load timeline'; parent.appendChild(errEl). This provides visual feedback without breaking the module UI.

_Source: `public/modules/learning-projects.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `error-handling`, `UX`, `patterns`, `DOM`_

## Module Detail/List View Pattern with Location.hash Routing

Frontend modules support both list and detail views by exporting mount(el, { params }) which inspects location.hash to determine which view to render. Helper functions renderList(el) and renderDetail(el, id) handle each view. When user clicks an item, update location.hash (e.g., #auto-evolves/pattern-123) to route to detail view. Module unmount() cleans up state.

_Source: `public/modules/auto-evolves.js, public/modules/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `routing`, `SPA`, `modules`, `view-management`_

## Chart.js Instance Lifecycle Management in SPA Modules

Modules using Chart.js (like learning-projects.js) maintain a charts array to track all instances. Before re-rendering or unmounting, call destroyCharts() which iterates the array, invokes destroy() on each Chart instance, and clears the array. This pattern prevents Canvas memory leaks and dangling references when modules are re-mounted or navigation changes the DOM.

_Source: `public/modules/learning-projects.js`_

_Tags: `chart`, `memory-management`, `lifecycle`, `spa`_

## Confidence Score Visualization as Percentage Bar

Frontend modules (daily-reviews, auto-evolves) visualize confidence scores (0-1) as percentage bars. Implement via confidenceBar(score) function that creates a bar div with CSS width (score * 100)% and a background color. Use opacity or hue variation (green for high, red for low) to indicate confidence intensity. Returns DOM node ready for insertion.

_Source: `public/modules/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `visualization`, `confidence`, `ui-pattern`, `accessibility`_

## Status Enum to Color Mapping for Visual Badges

Frontend modules map status enums to hex colors in an object (e.g., { active: '#6c5ce7', promoted: '#00b894', reverted: '#e17055' }). Apply this color to badge text or background. Ensures consistent visual status identification across list views (daily-reviews, auto-evolves) without CSS classname duplication.

_Source: `public/modules/auto-evolves.js`_

_Tags: `ui-pattern`, `color-scheme`, `status-display`_

## Systematic Column Removal from SPA Data Tables

When removing a column from paginated data tables in frontend modules: (1) Remove column name from header array, (2) Remove corresponding cell rendering from row construction loop, (3) Remove any dependent helper functions (e.g., badge renderers), (4) Verify and remove unused imports. Coordinate changes across all layers to prevent dangling code and incomplete refactoring.

_Source: `/Users/du/Workspace/open-pulse/public/modules/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `table`, `spa`, `refactoring`_

## Import Verification Before Removal

Before removing an imported function from a module, grep the file to verify no remaining usages of that function. Only remove the import if search returns zero matches. Prevents broken references and orphaned dependencies during refactoring.

_Source: `/Users/du/Workspace/open-pulse/public/modules/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `imports`, `refactoring`, `verification`_

## Utility Function Duplication Across Modules

Multiple utility functions are duplicated across different modules (slugify in op-auto-evolve.js and op-promote.js; parseFrontmatter in op-helpers.js and op-instinct-updater.js; isGitRepo in op-helpers.js and op-knowledge.js; getComponentPath in op-auto-evolve.js and op-promote.js; getClaudeDir in op-helpers.js and op-knowledge.js). This creates maintenance debt — changes to one copy don't propagate to others. Consider consolidating shared utilities into a single module (already exists as op-helpers.js in some cases) and importing them consistently across the codebase.

_Source: `src/op-auto-evolve.js, src/op-promote.js, src/op-helpers.js, src/op-knowledge.js, src/op-instinct-updater.js`_

_Tags: `refactoring`, `maintenance`, `duplication`, `backend`_

## Database Query Modules as Stateless Interfaces

The src/db/*.js modules (events.js, sessions.js, components.js, knowledge.js, knowledge-entries.js) contain pure database query functions with no imports or business logic. Each module is a stateless interface to a specific table or query domain. These modules receive the database instance as a parameter and return query results. This pattern allows clean separation between database access and application logic, making queries reusable and testable in isolation.

_Source: `src/db/`_

_Tags: `architecture`, `database`, `backend`, `query-pattern`_

## Hash-Based SPA Routing with Dynamic Module Imports

The router.js uses hash-based routing with dynamic ES6 imports to load route modules on demand. Each route is defined in ROUTES with a lazy import function (e.g., 'dashboard': () => import('./dashboard.js')). When the route changes, the router dynamically imports and renders the corresponding module. This pattern keeps the initial bundle small and enables code-splitting per route without a build step, relying on native ES6 module support in modern browsers.

_Source: `public/modules/router.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `routing`, `performance`, `es6-modules`_

## Large Comprehensive Integration Test Files

Integration test files are single, large files covering entire feature domains (op-knowledge.test.js with 918 lines for knowledge extraction + vault rendering; op-server.test.js with 806 lines for all HTTP endpoints; op-ingest.test.js with 448 lines for the full ingestion pipeline). These large test files use Node.js built-in test runner with describe/it blocks and test the entire feature workflow including database state, external API calls (Claude CLI), and side effects. This approach ensures integration coverage but creates large, potentially slow test files that should be organized with clear section comments.

_Source: `test/op-knowledge.test.js, test/op-server.test.js`_

_Tags: `testing`, `integration-testing`, `backend`_

## Module Complexity Threshold and Refactoring Signals

Modules exceeding ~400-500 lines (op-knowledge.js: 642, op-server.js: 171, op-ingest.js: 328) handle multiple concerns and warrant careful refactoring. Use line count as a signal to extract helper functions or split responsibilities. Critical: these large modules require comprehensive test coverage due to complexity and impact surface.

_Tags: `maintainability`, `refactoring`, `complexity`, `testing`_

## Shared frontend fetch wrapper as dependency-free utility

public/modules/api.js (47 lines) is a minimal fetch wrapper with no imports that wraps all REST calls with BASE='/api'. Every frontend module uses this centralized utility, allowing global API behavior changes without touching individual features. The dependency-free design prevents circular imports and makes it a reliable foundation.

_Source: `public/modules/api.js`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `api-communication`, `shared-utility`_

## Comprehensive test suites for high-complexity modules

The largest test files indicate test-first rigor for complex domains: op-knowledge.test.js (918 lines) validates 642-line op-knowledge.js with extensive scenario coverage; op-server.test.js (806 lines) validates API behavior. High test-to-code ratios (10:1 or higher for knowledge/server) signal important business logic that requires exhaustive validation.

_Source: `test/`_

_Tags: `testing`, `complexity`, `validation`, `coverage`_

## Frontend feature modules are self-contained implementations

Each public/modules/*.js file (dashboard, knowledge, inventory, prompts, projects, auto-evolves, daily-reviews) is a complete feature implementation with minimal cross-module dependencies. This pattern allows developers to understand a feature in isolation, add new features without touching others, and test UI updates independently.

_Source: `public/modules/`_

_Tags: `frontend`, `module-design`, `feature-isolation`_

## Pure database query modules pattern

All database query modules in src/db/ (events.js, sessions.js, components.js, knowledge-entries.js, knowledge.js) are pure functions with zero external imports. Each module focuses exclusively on database operations for a specific domain. This isolation pattern makes database logic easy to test, modify, and reason about independently from business logic.

_Source: `src/db/`_

_Tags: `database`, `module-design`, `isolation`, `testing`_

## Cold-Start Seeding Idempotency

Cold start seeding installs 10 universal starter instincts + CLAUDE.md rule parser. Idempotent — skips existing files on reinstall.

_Source: `CLAUDE.md`_

Internal operations signal themselves via OPEN_PULSE_INTERNAL environment variable to distinguish internal vs user-initiated actions.
